github_integration.overview: Guidelines for using GitHub MCP to interact with pull requests, fetch PR details, and manage the vulnerability remediation workflow through GitHub.

capability.name: fetch_assigned_prs
capability.description: Retrieve pull requests assigned to the user

use_cases.use_case: List all PRs where user is assigned as reviewer
Check PR status and CI results

capability.typical_workflow: User asks: "What are the GitHub PRs assigned to me?" You use GitHub MCP to fetch and display the list

capability.name: fetch_pr_details
capability.description: Get detailed information about a specific pull request

capability.name: read_pr_diff
capability.description: Access the code changes in the pull request

what_to_analyze.item: Code modifications made by DevOps engineer

capability.name: fetch_linked_issues
capability.description: Retrieve issues linked to the PR
capability.value: Issues often contain: - CVE details and context - Security advisory information - Discussion about the vulnerability - Remediation requirements

scenario.name: user_asks_for_assigned_prs
scenario.user_query: What are the GitHub PRs assigned to me?

step.action: Use GitHub MCP to fetch assigned PRs

step.criteria: - PRs with failing tests - PRs related to security/CVE fixes - PRs where you're assigned as reviewer - Open PRs (not merged or closed)

step.format:
## Pull Requests Assigned to You
### PR #123: Upgrade Flask to fix CVE-2023-12345 - **Status**: ❌ Tests Failing - **Author**: devops-engineer - **Created**: 2 days ago - **Changes**: requirements.txt (Flask 2.0.1 → 2.3.3) - **Failing Tests**: 5 tests in test_app.py
### PR #124: Update dependencies for security - **Status**: ✅ Tests Passing - **Author**: security-team - **Created**: 1 week ago - **Changes**: Multiple package upgrades

step.action: Ask user which PR to work on

suggestions.suggest: Review PR #123 and fix failing tests
List all failing tests in PR #123

scenario.name: user_provides_pr_number
scenario.user_query: Review PR #123 and fix the failing unit tests

step.action: Extract PR number from user message
Fetch PR details via GitHub MCP
step.details_to_extract: - PR title and description - Changed files - Diff content - CI status - Comments

step.action: Use GitHub MCP with full URL

section.name: security_context
section.item: CVE ID (from title, description, or linked issues)

step.categorize: - Dependency files (requirements.txt, package.json, etc.) - Application code - Test files - Configuration files - Documentation

step.extract: - Which packages changed - Old versions - New versions - Direct vs transitive dependencies

step.understand: - What the DevOps engineer already fixed - What patterns they used - What might still need fixing

step.analyze: - Test setup changes - Mock updates - Assertion changes - New test requirements

green_lines.significance: - New imports - New function calls - New configuration - Additions made by DevOps engineer

red_lines.significance: - Old imports removed - Deprecated code removed - Old configuration removed - What the DevOps engineer thought needed changing

context_lines.use: Understand the surrounding code and how changes fit in

status.name: failing_tests
status.action: This is what you need to fix

status.name: build_failure
status.meaning: Code doesn't compile or dependencies can't be installed
status.action: May need to fix build issues before tests

status.name: linting_errors
status.action: May need to address after fixing tests

status.name: pending
status.action: Wait for results or check locally

status.name: passing
status.action: No action needed for this check

what_to_look_for.item: Are failures in specific test files
Did tests pass before the PR
Are there any error messages visible

type.name: devops_notes
type.description: DevOps engineer explaining what they tried or what's known
type.example: "I upgraded Flask but the tests are failing due to API changes. Need help fixing the application code."
type.value: Tells you what's already been attempted and what's needed

type.name: reviewer_feedback
type.description: Other reviewers pointing out issues or suggestions
type.example: "The import statements need updating for the new Flask version"
type.value: May provide hints about what needs fixing

type.name: bot_comments
type.description: Automated comments from CI/CD or security bots
type.example: "Security vulnerability CVE-2023-12345 will be fixed by this upgrade"
type.value: Confirms the security context and purpose

why_check_issues.reason: Issues often contain more context than the PR
May include CVE details and security advisories
Could have discussion about remediation approach

pattern.name: security_advisory
pattern.contains: - CVE details - Vulnerability description - Affected versions - Fixed versions - Workarounds if any

pattern.name: upgrade_tracking
pattern.contains: - Reason for upgrade - Expected changes - Migration notes - Testing requirements

critical_principle.rule: ALWAYS commit changes to the EXISTING PR branch, NEVER create a new branch
critical_principle.rationale: The PR already exists with a specific branch. Committing to that branch will: - Trigger the CI/CD workflow automatically - Update the PR with your fixes - Allow the workflow to re-run tests and assign reviewers
critical_principle.never: Never create a new branch like "pr-38" or "fix-tests"
critical_principle.always: Always use the branch name from the PR metadata (e.g., "fix/cve-2023-12345-flask")

step.how: From the PR metadata fetched via GitHub MCP, extract: - head.ref (the branch name) - head.repo (the repository, usually same as base)
step.example: If PR shows: "highorbit25 wants to merge 1 commit into main from fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" Then the branch is: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
step.critical: This is the branch you MUST commit to

step.format:
fix: resolve unit test failures after [package] upgrade
- Fixed import paths for [package] v[version] - Updated API calls to match new signatures - Replaced deprecated methods with recommended alternatives
Addresses failing tests in PR #[number] Related to CVE-[id]

step.action: Ensure you're on the correct branch
step.critical: Use the EXACT branch name from the PR, not a new branch name. This ensures your commit updates the existing PR.

step.example:
git commit -m "fix: resolve unit test failures after Flask upgrade
- Fixed import paths for Flask v3.1.0 - Updated test assertions to match new behavior
Addresses failing tests in PR #38 Related to CVE-2024-12345"

step.result: This will: - Update the PR with your commit - Trigger the CI/CD workflow (.github/workflows/pr-unit-test.yml) - Re-run the unit tests - Automatically assign reviewers based on test results
step.critical: The branch name MUST match the PR's head branch exactly. Do NOT push to a different branch or create a new PR.

pr_comment.when: After pushing fixes to the PR branch
pr_comment.purpose: Document what was fixed and why for the PR reviewers
pr_comment.how: Use GitHub MCP to add a comment to the PR
pr_comment.tool: use_mcp_tool with github server and add_issue_comment tool

step.action: Add comment to PR using GitHub MCP

pr_comment.template:
## Unit Test Fixes
I've fixed the failing unit tests caused by the [package] upgrade.
### Changes Made - Updated import statements in [files] - Modified API calls to match new [package] v[version] signatures - Replaced deprecated `old_method()` with `new_method()`
### Root Cause The upgrade from v[old] to v[new] introduced these breaking changes: - [Breaking change 1] - [Breaking change 2]
### Testing - All [N] unit tests now pass ✅ - No regressions introduced - Security fix verified and maintained
### Files Modified - `[file1]`: [reason] - `[file2]`: [reason]
Ready for review!

why_this_matters.reason: Documents the remediation work for reviewers
Explains the root cause of test failures
Shows what was changed and why
Provides context for the Software Lead review

practice.name: clear_communication
practice.principle: Communicate clearly with PR author and reviewers

guidelines.guideline: Explain what you fixed and why
Note any decisions or trade-offs made
Ask questions if anything is unclear

practice.name: respect_pr_scope
practice.principle: Stay within the scope of the PR

guidelines.guideline: Fix only test failures related to the upgrade
Don't add unrelated features or refactoring
Don't change the security fix itself

practice.name: document_decisions
practice.principle: Document why you made specific choices

guidelines.guideline: Explain non-obvious fixes in code comments

issue.name: cannot_fetch_pr
issue.symptom: GitHub MCP fails to fetch PR details

possible_causes.cause: PR is in a private repository without access

solutions.solution: Wait and retry if rate limited
Ask user to provide PR details manually

possible_causes.cause: PR is very large with many changes
Some information not available via API
Permissions limiting what can be accessed

solutions.solution: Ask user for specific missing information
Check PR directly on GitHub web interface
